title: श्री विश्वकर्मा चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विश्वकर्मा चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥

श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु चरण, वन्दन करूँ प्रणाम।
शिल्प कला के देवता, पूरण करो सब काम॥

जय विश्वकर्मा देवा, जगत रचैया मेवा।
तव शरणागत आज हम, करो कृपा की सेवा॥

॥ चौपाई ॥

विश्वकर्मा प्रभु दिव्य शरीरा। सकल सृष्टि के तुम्हीं अधीरा॥
ब्रह्मा पुत्र महान तुम देवा। सब देवन ने करी तव सेवा॥ १॥

स्वर्ग लोक तुमने रचे भाई। लंकापुरी अति सुन्दर बनाई॥
द्वारका नगरी तुमने सजाई। इन्द्रप्रस्थ की शोभा बढ़ाई॥ २॥

सुदर्शन चक्र तुमने बनाया। त्रिशूल महादेव को दे आया॥
वज्र इन्द्र का तुमने रचा है। पुष्पक विमान विधा रची है॥ ३॥

सकल यन्त्र तुम्हारी रचना। सकल तन्त्र भी तुम्हारी सजना॥
शिल्पकला के ज्ञाता तुम हो। मन्दिर भवन बनाता तुम हो॥ ४॥

पञ्च मुख हैं तुम्हारे स्वामी। सद्योजात ईशान अन्तर्यामी॥
वामदेव अघोर तत्पुरुषा। तुम्हीं हो सब विधि का पुरुषा॥ ५॥

मनु मय त्वष्टा विश्वज्ञ शिल्पी। दैवज्ञ पाँचों पुत्र तुम्हारे॥
लोहा काष्ठ ताम्र शिल्पकारी। स्वर्णकार तव वंशज प्यारे॥ ६॥

कारीगरों के ईश्वर तुम हो। श्रमिकों के आधार तुम हो॥
यन्त्र विद्या के प्रथम गुरु तुम। वास्तु शास्त्र के मूल ऋषि तुम॥ ७॥

विश्वकर्मा जयन्ती मनाते। शिल्पी लोग तुम्हें शीश नवाते॥
यन्त्र औजारों की पूजा होती। तव कृपा से सब सिद्धि होती॥ ८॥

रक्षा करो श्रमिकों की स्वामी। दुर्घटना से बचाओ अन्तर्यामी॥
उद्योग धन्धे में उन्नति दो। कौशल कर्म में शक्ति भरो॥ ९॥

तुम्हरी कृपा से यन्त्र चले सब। तव आशीष से सफल हो सब॥
जो तेरी पूजा नित्य करे वो। जीवन में सब सुख पावे वो॥ १०॥

॥ दोहा ॥

विश्वकर्मा चालीसा यह, पढ़े जो प्रेम लगाय।
शिल्प कर्म में सिद्धि हो, सब विपदा मिट जाय॥

॥ इति श्री विश्वकर्मा चालीसा सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishwakarma Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||

Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu charan, vandan karun pranam |
Shilpa kala ke devata, puran karo sab kam ||

Jai Vishwakarma Deva, jagat rachaiya meva |
Tav sharanagat aaj hum, karo kripa ki seva ||

|| Chaupai ||

Vishwakarma Prabhu divya sharira, sakal srishti ke tumhi adhira |
Brahma putra mahan tum Deva, sab devan ne kari tav seva || 1 ||

Swarg lok tumne rache bhai, Lankapuri ati sundar banai |
Dwaraka nagri tumne sajai, Indraprasth ki shobha badhai || 2 ||

Sudarshan chakra tumne banaya, Trishul Mahadev ko de aaya |
Vajra Indra ka tumne racha hai, Pushpak viman vidha rachi hai || 3 ||

Sakal yantra tumhari rachna, sakal tantra bhi tumhari sajna |
Shilpakala ke gyata tum ho, mandir bhavan banata tum ho || 4 ||

Panch mukh hain tumhare Swami, Sadyojat Ishan antaryami |
Vamadev Aghor Tatpurusha, tumhi ho sab vidhi ka Purusha || 5 ||

Manu Maya Tvashta Vishwajna Shilpi, Daivagya panchon putra tumhare |
Loha kashtha tamra shilpakari, swarnkar tav vanshaj pyare || 6 ||

Karigaron ke Ishwar tum ho, shramikon ke adhar tum ho |
Yantra vidya ke pratham guru tum, Vastu Shastra ke mool rishi tum || 7 ||

Vishwakarma Jayanti manate, shilpi log tumhein shish navate |
Yantra aujaron ki puja hoti, tav kripa se sab siddhi hoti || 8 ||

Raksha karo shramikon ki Swami, durghatna se bachao antaryami |
Udyog dhandhe mein unnati do, kaushal karma mein shakti bharo || 9 ||

Tumhari kripa se yantra chale sab, tav ashish se safal ho sab |
Jo teri puja nitya kare vo, jivan mein sab sukh pave vo || 10 ||

|| Doha ||

Vishwakarma Chalisa yah, padhe jo prem lagay |
Shilpa karma mein siddhi ho, sab vipada mit jay ||

|| Iti Shri Vishwakarma Chalisa Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: यह चालीसा विश्वकर्मा भगवान की स्तुति है, जो देवताओं के शिल्पकार और सृष्टि के निर्माता हैं। उन्होंने स्वर्ग लोक, लंका, द्वारका और इन्द्रप्रस्थ का निर्माण किया। सुदर्शन चक्र, त्रिशूल, वज्र और पुष्पक विमान उनकी रचनाएँ हैं। उनके पाँच मुख (सद्योजात, ईशान, वामदेव, अघोर, तत्पुरुष) हैं। सभी कारीगर और श्रमिक उनकी सन्तान माने जाते हैं। यह चालीसा शिल्प कर्म में सिद्धि, दुर्घटना से रक्षा और उद्योग में उन्नति के लिए पढ़ी जाती है।
benefits: Invokes mastery and skill in craftsmanship and engineering
Protects workers and artisans from workplace accidents
Promotes success in business and industry
Blesses tools and machinery for safe operation
Invokes the blessing of creativity and innovation in one's profession
Traditionally associated with prosperity to craftsmen and their families
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Vishwakarma Chalisa (श्री विश्वकर्मा चालीसा) stands as a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn. Attributed to Traditional Vishwakarma Bhakti Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bring prosperity to craftsmen and their families and grant mastery and skill in craftsmanship and engineering. It is also said to promote success in business and industry. For best results, devotees chant this on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Vishwakarma Jayanti. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Vishwakarma Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishwakarma Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Prayer to divine architect Lord Vishwakarma for skill and prosperity.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Vishwakarma and why is the Chalisa addressed to him?
seoFaqs.answer: Vishwakarma is the divine architect of the cosmos in Vedic and Puranic tradition — designer of the gods' palaces, the city of Dwarka for Krishna, the golden Lanka for Ravana, Indraprastha for the Pandavas, the Sudarshana Chakra, Pushpaka Vimana, and Indra's Vajra. The Chalisa is his 40-verse devotional stuti, reciting his role as the patron deity of architects, engineers, craftsmen, mechanics, and all skilled labour.

seoFaqs.question: When is Vishwakarma Chalisa traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal day is Vishwakarma Jayanti / Vishwakarma Puja, observed on Kanya Sankranti (typically 17 September) when the sun enters Virgo. Factories, workshops, vehicles, machines and tools are worshipped on this day. It is also recited on Diwali Vishwakarma Puja (the day after Diwali in some regions), and weekly on Sundays by craftsmen, engineers, and industrialists.

seoFaqs.question: Who recites the Vishwakarma Chalisa most commonly?
seoFaqs.answer: It is the patron prayer of the Vishwakarma community (encompassing five traditional artisan castes — Sutar/carpenters, Lohar/blacksmiths, Tamrakar/coppersmiths, Sonar/goldsmiths, and Shilpkar/sculptors), as well as modern engineers, architects, mechanics, factory workers, drivers, IT professionals, and business owners. It is recited before starting any new construction, vehicle, machine, or workshop.

seoFaqs.question: What is the standard Vishwakarma Puja sequence with the Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Place the Vishwakarma murti or photo facing east, decorate workshop / tools / vehicles with flowers and a yellow-red cloth, light a ghee diya and dhoop, offer akshat, haldi-kumkum, yellow flowers and sweets (peda or laddu). Recite the Vishwakarma Chalisa, then the Vishwakarma Aarti 'Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu', and conclude by tying a kalava on each tool / machine. Distribute prasad to all workers.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does Vishwakarma Chalisa promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional phalashruti promises smooth functioning of machinery and tools (no breakdowns), workplace safety, growth of skill (kaushal-vriddhi), business expansion for craftsmen and industrialists, success in technical projects, and protection of vehicles from accidents. The Chalisa also teaches the dharmic value of skilled labour — that work done with devotion and integrity is itself a form of yajna.
